briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now commence the climate briefing for June 6, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis and Threats to Human Health and Livelihoods
sections.content: Europe is experiencing record-breaking heatwaves with daytime temperatures reaching 35-40 degrees Celsius, and warnings have been issued that this 'European heat dome' could trigger heavy rainfall and heatwaves on the Korean Peninsula [1]. Such climate change directly impacts sports events, posing increasing risks to World Cup athletes [13], and has also been cited as potentially tripling the burden of cardiovascular diseases by 2050 [14]. Furthermore, even the rich environment of specific regions like the Coachella Valley is threatened by climate change [17]. In the UK, ministers have been criticized for complacency regarding risks to food supply due to fuel shortages, cyberattacks, and extreme weather, leading to growing calls for food security to become an immediate national priority [22].

sections.title: ⚠️ Climate Change Reshaping Industrial and Cultural Landscapes
sections.content: Climate change is directly impacting specific industrial sectors, with wine growers, in particular, facing situations where they may need to change grape varieties [18]. Amidst this crisis, Sicily is demonstrating adaptation efforts by serving as an outdoor laboratory to study technology and tradition in the wine industry under climate crisis conditions [15]. In the sports sector, reports indicate increasing risks to World Cup athletes due to climate change [13], illustrating how climate change is transforming existing frameworks across various aspects of our lives. This suggests that it is not merely an environmental issue but demands significant structural changes across the economy, culture, and society as a whole.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Across South Korea
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to spread a culture of carbon neutrality are underway nationwide. Sejong City and Chungcheongnam-do are promoting carbon neutrality transition forums and the dissemination of practical culture through Environmental Education Week and Environment Day ceremonies [2, 5]. Geumsan Office of Education and Incheon City also conducted carbon neutrality campaigns with citizens to mark World Environment Day [4, 8]. Seosan Daesan High School is encouraging youth participation in carbon neutrality through future-oriented education, with students actively engaging in 'Carbon Zero Day' events [3, 7]. Furthermore, the Korea Racing Authority is leading carbon neutrality efforts by creating a 'Kenaf Garden,' and Chungcheongbuk-do has secured 20.4 billion KRW in national funding for public building green remodeling projects to advance carbon neutrality and enhance energy efficiency [6, 11]. Various local governments, including Jeollanam-do, are collaborating with both the private and public sectors to expand carbon neutrality practices [9].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Dialogue and the Pursuit of Energy Transition
sections.content: In the international community, various discussions and efforts to address climate change are ongoing. The First Lady of Türkiye emphasized the importance of the zero-waste movement as a method to combat climate change, stating that multiple approaches should be combined [19, 21]. Meanwhile, an environmental organization in Oakville is working to 're-engage dialogue on climate change' [20], and news that key climate change figures are confronting budgetary realities underscores the importance of developing sustainable policies [16]. In the energy sector, domestic organizations including the Korean Nuclear Society, the Korean Society for New and Renewable Energy, and the Korean Institute of Electrical Engineers have issued joint policy recommendations for the 12th Basic Plan for Long-term Electricity Supply and Demand, exploring directions for energy transition [10]. Vietnam is also gaining opportunities to make breakthroughs in the renewable energy sector, thus joining the international trend of energy transition [12].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, alongside various response efforts unfolding both domestically and internationally. We reiterate the importance of sustained attention and action regarding climate change, and with that, we conclude today's climate briefing.
